title: coreopsis cardaminifolia
intro: Coreopsis cardaminifolia, a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States, is a member of the sunflower family (Asteraceae) commonly known as tickseed. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Coreopsis cardaminifolia has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is also used in herbal supplements, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
